# spark-mcp

> Read-only MCP server for Spark Desktop email -- queries local SQLite databases directly, no IMAP or API keys needed.

## What It Does
Gives Claude (or any MCP client) read-only access to Spark email on macOS. Search messages, read threads, browse contacts and folders -- all from Spark's local cache with zero network access.

## Install
```bash
git clone https://github.com/bbdaniels/spark-mcp.git
cd spark-mcp && pip install -e .
claude mcp add spark-email -- spark-mcp
```

## Tools
- `spark_list_accounts` -- List configured email accounts
- `spark_search` -- Full-text search across all accounts (supports FTS5 syntax)
- `spark_get_message` -- Read a specific message with body and attachments
- `spark_get_conversation` -- Read an entire email thread
- `spark_recent` -- List recent inbox messages
- `spark_search_contacts` -- Search contacts by name or email
- `spark_list_folders` -- List folders/labels per account

## Notes
- **Read-only**: All database access uses SQLite read-only mode
- **macOS only**: Requires Spark Desktop installed locally
- **No credentials**: Uses Spark's existing local SQLite cache
- **No network**: Zero external connections -- everything is local disk reads
